full_response:
Let King Arthur’s inertial frame be K.  
In that frame

• Knight #2 moves with constant speed v (>0) in the +x–direction.  
  (Hence Knight #2’s world-line is x₂(t) = v t.)

• Knight #2’s “starting line’’ is a post fixed on the ground at the position x = L
  (so its world-line is x = L, all t).

Event E₂ is defined as “Knight #2 is exactly on his own starting line.”  
Therefore E₂ is the intersection of the two world-lines

  x₂(t) = v t  and  x = L .

Setting them equal gives the time of the event in Arthur’s frame:

  v t_K  =  L  ⇒ t_K  =  L / v .

Substituting this back into either world-line yields the position:

  x_K = v t_K = v (L / v) = L .

Thus, in King Arthur’s coordinates,

  t_K = L / v ,  x_K = L .

\boxed{(t_K ,\, x_K) \;=\;\bigl(L/v ,\; L\bigr)}

Confidence: 23%
